name: YARA (Malware Pattern Matching)

auth.notes: Local library; VirusTotal API for cloud scanning uses API key

performance.notes: C library with Python bindings; very fast scanning even for complex rules

agent_readiness.notes: The standard tool for malware identification and pattern matching. Write rules with string/regex/byte patterns + boolean conditions. Match against files, processes, memory. Rules can import modules (PE, ELF, Mach-O, math, hash). YARA rules are portable and shareable. Used by antivirus vendors, threat intel teams, incident response. VirusTotal, CAPEsandbox, Cuckoo, Velociraptor all support YARA. Python bindings via yara-python.

gotchas: YARA rule writing requires security expertise
High false positive rates with weak rules
C library must be installed separately from Python bindings
Performance degrades with thousands of complex rules
best_when: Threat hunting across file systems and memory
Security tool pipelines (EDR, SIEM, sandbox integration)
avoid_when: Teams without threat intelligence/malware analysis background
General-purpose file filtering (simpler regex approaches better)
summary: YARA is the standard malware pattern matching language and tool. Write rules with string/byte patterns and boolean conditions to identify malware families. Scans files, processes, and memory. Supported by VirusTotal, Cuckoo, CAPE, Velociraptor. Python bindings via yara-python. Used by threat intel teams, incident responders, and AV vendors. Essential for malware analysis and threat hunting.
